content_templates: Welcome to the {{department}} team at Acme Corp! This guide will help you get started during your first {{number}} weeks. Your manager {{manager_name}} will be your primary point of contact.

Before your first day, make sure you have access to the following systems: {{system_list}}. If you run into access issues, file a ticket in ServiceNow under the '{{department}} Access Requests' category.

During your first week, you should complete the mandatory compliance training modules at https://acmecorp.internal/training. Your buddy {{buddy_name}} from the {{department}} team will walk you through the team's daily standup process and introduce you to key stakeholders.

Acme Corp's {{service_name}} is built on a microservices architecture deployed on {{cloud_provider}}. The system handles approximately {{number}} requests per second at peak load and maintains {{uptime}}% uptime SLA.

The core data flow is: Client -> API Gateway ({{gateway_name}}) -> {{service_name}} -> {{database_type}} (primary) with {{cache_type}} caching layer. All inter-service communication uses gRPC with mTLS for encryption in transit.

For disaster recovery, we maintain active-passive replication across {{region_primary}} and {{region_secondary}} regions. RPO is {{number}} minutes and RTO is {{number}} minutes. Refer to the Runbook for failover procedures.

The {{service_name}} API provides RESTful endpoints for managing {{resource_type}} resources. Base URL: https://api.acmecorp.internal/{{service_slug}}/v{{version}}. All requests require a valid Bearer token obtained from the OAuth2 /token endpoint.

POST /{{resource_slug}} - Creates a new {{resource_type}}. Required fields: name (string), owner_id (uuid), department (enum). Optional fields: description (string, max {{number}} chars), tags (array of strings), priority (enum: low|medium|high|critical).

Rate limits: Authenticated requests are limited to {{number}} requests per minute per API key. Batch endpoints support up to {{number}} items per request. For higher limits, contact the Platform team via #platform-support.

Deployments to production follow the standard CI/CD pipeline defined in .gitlab-ci.yml. All merges to main trigger an automated build, test suite execution, and container image push to our internal registry at registry.acmecorp.internal/{{service_slug}}.

Production deployments require at least {{number}} approvals in GitLab and a passing security scan. Rolling deployments are the default strategy with {{number}}% canary traffic for the first {{number}} minutes. Rollback is automatic if error rate exceeds {{number}}%.

Post-deployment verification checklist: (1) Confirm new pods are healthy in Kubernetes dashboard, (2) Check Datadog dashboards for {{service_name}} error rates, (3) Verify smoke tests pass at https://smoke.acmecorp.internal/{{service_slug}}, (4) Update the deployment log in Confluence.
All Acme Corp employees in {{department}} must adhere to the following security requirements. These policies are reviewed quarterly by the Security team and were last updated on {{date}}.

Data classification levels at Acme Corp: PUBLIC (marketing materials, blog posts), INTERNAL (team docs, Slack messages), CONFIDENTIAL (customer data, financial reports), RESTRICTED (credentials, security audit findings, executive compensation). Handle each according to the Data Handling Matrix.

Incident response: If you suspect a security incident, immediately notify #security on Slack and page the on-call security engineer via PagerDuty. Do not attempt to investigate or remediate on your own. Preserve all logs and evidence. The Security team lead {{security_lead}} will coordinate the response.

All code changes at Acme Corp require peer review before merging. The {{department}} team follows these guidelines to ensure high-quality, maintainable, and secure code across all {{number}}+ repositories.

Review checklist: (1) Correctness - does the logic match the requirements in the Jira ticket? (2) Security - no hardcoded secrets, SQL injection vectors, or XSS vulnerabilities. (3) Performance - no N+1 queries, appropriate indexing, bounded memory usage. (4) Testing - unit tests cover edge cases, integration tests for API changes.

Turnaround expectations: Reviews should be completed within {{number}} business hours. If a review is blocking a release, escalate in #{{department_slug}}-reviews. For changes affecting shared libraries, require an additional review from the Platform team.

The {{department}} team maintains a testing pyramid: {{number}}% unit tests, {{number}}% integration tests, {{number}}% end-to-end tests. Our target coverage is {{number}}% for critical paths and {{number}}% overall.

Unit tests use {{test_framework}} and must run in under {{number}} seconds per test file. Mocking external dependencies is mandatory -- no network calls in unit tests. Use the shared fixtures in tests/conftest.py for common setup.

E2E tests run nightly in our staging environment against staging.acmecorp.internal. Test results are posted to #{{department_slug}}-quality each morning. Flaky test owners are tagged automatically and have {{number}} business days to fix or quarantine the test.

Discussion notes: {{facilitator_name}} raised the topic of {{topic}} and the team agreed to {{resolution}}. {{dissenter_name}} raised concerns about {{concern}} which will be addressed in a follow-up spike tracked in JIRA-{{ticket_number}}.
Resource allocation: {{number}} engineers, {{number}} designer(s), {{number}} PM. Total estimated effort: {{number}} person-weeks. Budget approved by {{approver_name}} on {{date}}.

This page documents the agreed-upon best practices for the {{department}} team at Acme Corp. These guidelines were established based on retrospectives and incident post-mortems from Q{{quarter}} {{year}}.

Communication norms: Use #{{department_slug}} for general discussion, #{{department_slug}}-alerts for automated notifications, and #{{department_slug}}-support for cross-team requests. Tag @{{department_slug}}-oncall for urgent issues outside business hours.

Documentation standards: All new features must have a corresponding Confluence page under the {{department}} space. Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) are required for decisions affecting system boundaries, data models, or external APIs. Use the ADR template at /templates/adr.

This playbook covers common incident scenarios for {{service_name}} and their resolution steps. Severity levels: SEV1 (complete outage, all hands), SEV2 (partial degradation, on-call + backup), SEV3 (minor issue, on-call only).

The {{department}} data pipeline ingests data from {{number}} sources and processes approximately {{number}} events per day. The pipeline runs on Apache Airflow with DAGs scheduled at various intervals depending on data freshness requirements.

Data sources: {{source_list}}. Each source has a dedicated ingestion connector that handles schema validation, deduplication, and dead-letter routing. Failed records are sent to the DLQ in {{queue_service}} for manual review.

Data quality checks run after each pipeline stage. We monitor for: null rates exceeding {{number}}%, schema drift, row count anomalies (>{{number}}% deviation from baseline), and freshness SLA violations. Alerts go to #{{department_slug}}-data-quality.

This document summarizes the {{department}} team's evaluation of {{vendor_category}} vendors for Acme Corp. Evaluation period: {{start_date}} to {{end_date}}. Evaluation committee: {{evaluator_list}}.

Evaluation criteria (weighted): Functionality ({{number}}%), Security & Compliance ({{number}}%), Cost ({{number}}%), Integration Ease ({{number}}%), Support Quality ({{number}}%). Each vendor scored 1-5 on each criterion.

Recommendation: Based on the scoring matrix, we recommend {{recommended_vendor}} with a total weighted score of {{score}}/5.0. Key strengths: {{strengths}}. Key risks: {{risks}}. Procurement to begin pending {{approver_name}} approval.

Objective 1: {{objective_1}}
- KR1: {{kr_1}} (target: {{target_1}}, current: {{current_1}})
- KR2: {{kr_2}} (target: {{target_2}}, current: {{current_2}})
- KR3: {{kr_3}} (target: {{target_3}}, current: {{current_3}})

Objective 2: {{objective_2}}
- KR1: {{kr_4}} (target: {{target_4}}, current: {{current_4}})
- KR2: {{kr_5}} (target: {{target_5}}, current: {{current_5}})

Owner: {{okr_owner}}. Weekly check-in: {{meeting_day}} at {{meeting_time}}.
Dependencies on other teams: {{dependency_list}}. Risks to delivery: {{risk_list}}. Mitigation plans documented in the associated project plans linked above.

Exceptions: If {{exception_condition}}, escalate to {{escalation_contact}} in the {{department}} team. Exceptions must be documented in the process exception log and reviewed during the next quarterly audit.

Welcome to Acme Corp! This checklist ensures you have everything set up for a productive start in the {{department}} team.

Day 1:
- [ ] Laptop setup and VPN configuration
- [ ] Slack workspace access (join #general, #{{department_slug}})
- [ ] 1:1 with manager {{manager_name}}

Week 1:
- [ ] Complete security awareness training
- [ ] Set up development environment (see {{department}} Dev Setup Guide)
- [ ] Read the {{department}} Architecture Overview
- [ ] Attend team standup and sprint planning
- [ ] Meet your onboarding buddy {{buddy_name}}

First 30 days:
- [ ] Complete {{number}} starter tickets from the backlog
- [ ] Shadow an on-call rotation
- [ ] Present a short intro at the {{department}} team all-hands
- [ ] Provide feedback on the onboarding process to {{hr_contact}}
